summary: Serializes a value and its nested contents into formatted query-parameter fragments using configurable encoding, traversal, and null-handling options.
signature_intent: Accepts a value, its current query key context, and serialization options, then returns an array of query-string fragments or a bare key for strict null serialization.
behavior: Applies an optional transformation callback before normal serialization.
Converts Date values with the supplied date serializer.
In comma-array mode, converts Date elements and joins array contents with commas before encoding.
Serializes primitive values and binary buffers as single key-value fragments.
Uses the configured encoding and formatting callbacks for keys and values when provided.
Traverses objects and arrays recursively to produce fragments for nested values.
Uses either bracket notation or dot notation for nested object keys, depending on configuration.
Uses the configured array key strategy when traversing arrays.
Can restrict traversal to an explicit key list.
Can sort discovered keys with a supplied comparison callback.
Can omit properties whose value is null when null skipping is enabled.
edge_cases: A null value returns a bare encoded key in strict null mode.
A null value becomes an empty string outside strict null mode.
An undefined value produces no fragments.
Without an encoding callback, raw string conversion is used for primitive values.
In value-only encoding mode, keys are formatted but not encoded.
Empty traversable values produce an empty fragment list.
forbidden_details: Source locations and project metadata are omitted.
